document_metadata.title: ** Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Single Crystals
document_metadata.author: ** James I. Bryant
document_metadata.page_source: Bryant - 1964 - Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Single Crystals 2-02

sample_description:
- **Document Title:** Vibrational Spectrum of Sodium Azide Single Crystals
- **Author:** James I. Bryant
- **Year and Publication Details:** Published in The Journal of Chemical Physics, Volume 40, Number 11, on June 1, 1964.
- **Sample Description and Experimental Conditions:** The study focuses on a single crystal of sodium azide (NaN₃). Infrared and Raman spectra were obtained at temperatures of 298°K and 90°K using grating spectrometers.
- **Spectroscopic Technique Used:** Infrared and Raman
overall_findings:
ell modes to irreducible representations.
  - Medium-strength absorptions were observed, and tentative assignments were made for all bands.
- **Overall Findings or Conclusions from the Page:** 
  - The spectra showed medium-strength absorptions and confirmed the assignment of medium-strength bands as sum and difference frequencies involving rotational motion of the azide ion as a rigid unit.
  - Observations were in good agreement with the accepted structure described by the space group D₃d—R3m.
